text: TMobile has horrible service and customer service. I get a discount for setting my account on auto-pay but what they don’t tell you is that gives them permission to charge you any amounts they want to. Since my service was so bad, they shipped me a cell spot tower, which I was told was free of charge, and by the way the cell spot still didn’t fix my service. I’ve had this cell spot for over 1 year and only used it for 6 months because it stopped working all the way. A few months ago I canceled the data service on my tablet because that stopped working as well and not once was I told that the tower was linked to that tablet number and if I canceled it I would get charged $105 for the tower, the customer service agent failed to mention that when she shut the data on the tablet off. Well they took that amount out of my bank account without my permission, thieves. Now I have to inconveniently take time away from work to go to UPS and drop the box off and wait 7-10 days for my refund, T-MOBILE IS A BUNCH OF NON-WORKING NETWORK SCAMMING THIEVES...BEWARE.

IF YOUR THINKING ABOUT GETTING TMOBILE DO NOT GET IT..... 

Catagory 5 ⚠️☣☢

they literally will chargeyou if even when the bill is overdue it keeps
Adding every month , 
They charged me for  2 months the phone been turnt off
after they will charge an extra 250 after the 18 month lease.........i called them
The agents seem very nice , they dont help solve the problem at all.... in 987.90 in the hole because of procesed bill  for a phone that havent been turnt on

Dont get tmobile 

if you want life debt

Have at it

If I could give a minus five I would. For 3 years I struggled making calls while at work (I work 10 hour shifts) and because this was at my office, T-Mobile wouldn't send me a booster...for three years I was promised they were growing in our area. I live in and work in the city of green bay. After being fed up with the plan and cancelling they proceeded to send out a booster in error, Like Seriously???....You'll were horrible and your customer service leaves something to be desired....

Awful coverage. Awful customer service.
Minimal coverage in Boston and Atlanta. Dropped calls all along the I-95 corridor.
Long lines. Conflicting answers.
And TMobile Tuesdays is a joke. If there is a thank you gift the store is either out or you stand on line for 30 minutes. Or you stand in line for 30 minutes to be told they are out. ( Before noon)
Horrible, Horrible, Horrible.

If anyone goes with t mobile just an fyi there provider sucks . Save your money and go to cricket or Verizon or a t and t

DONT TRUST T MOBILE
I been with t mobile more then 8 years but every year they getting worse and worse they always lie and charge u more then they should and never take the blame on mistakes they do worst customer service other companies charge cheaper then them with much better services and DO NOT TRUST THEM with auto pay just so u can get a few bucks off each month they will charge you random amounts and at the end when you call even if they wrong you not getting the money back I called my bank to do a charge back

If I could give no star I would. I m T-Mobile customer for 18 years and so dissatisfied with the company customer service. I will give full details what happened that no one goes through the same issue ever. In February 2018 I have paid my monthly bill through my bank’s checking account.  The amount was posted on my bank statement Feb12, 2018. For some reason the payment was never  posted on my T mobile account. In March 2018 I have received a call from t mobile rep that my account is jeopardized because I did not pay Feb bill. To ensure that my service is not interrupted I have authorized my payment for Feb and March 2018 through my credit card.  Of course I trusted t mobile rep and did not check if the issued check in February cleared up. I have noticed that I have payed twice for February 2018. Once I discovered the issue I called T mobile and this issue is still not resolved. I have issued all requested documents to T mobile billing department to support the claim and there is always some type of excuse from T mobile rep NOT TO return my money back. I spoke with billing supervisors , managers ( every call different rep handles this issue) and still have not received my payment back. My bank issued requested documents: checking account bank statements and cleared check with t mobile’s stamp on the back confirming cashed check by T mobile and unfortunately they don’t want to return my money back . I really don’t know what to do next.. they don’t give me any other choice but to go through the small court claim and try to fight the payment back. T mobile customer service stinks. The customer service reps are not helpful, unpleasant and useless. I hope no one ever has to go through what I am going through.

I opened a business account with Tmobile Worst business choice! their Internet is always down, the calls drop constantly. I called 40 days ago to complain about this they said they would fix it. I called again this week spoke to two service agents Elly was one who could not speak English both with heavy accents could not communicate. i asked for a supervision I was put on hold for a long long time. Called again they could not help me I lost clients time and effor trusting T-mobile that is in the same network as metro pcs was a horrible experience and a waist of time and money.
We recently switched from T-mobile to Verizon. It took 4 days, 7 calls to T-mobile, and hours on the phone to get our phones released. So frustrating!!!

If I could give -5 stars I would . They called my on my day off offering me a great deal buy 1 get 1 free I watch . I made sure I confirmed with lady 3 times even asked for an email to confirm I had received offer . Never received email , I did receive watches over the mail . I did not open them . I called another 2 times to get email proof that I had received deal because deal was not coming out on my online bill . The 3rd time I called got transferred to 2 people and they said unfortunately they did not know which deal I was talking about . I’m so disgusted with their tactics of making u sign a purchase just to make a sale . Never again . They can go .....

T-Mobile is a big thief. I switched over to Verizon August 2018 and because I was doing auto pay t-mobile took my money until November 2018 when  I detected it. I went to the store, called them up several times to get my money back instead I was told according to their policy they don't give refunds but I can go through my bank to get my money back which I did and got only part of it. The next thing I know collections start harassing me for the amount t-mobile owes me. I had disputed it with them and the collections but different collections started calling again. T-mobile would not stop. I am sick of this back and forth thing I think the next thing would be to take T-mobile to court. I am furious!

I travel overseas often for months at a time.  T-Mobile used to be the only company that would allow me to drop my cell plan to $3 a month to keep my phone number active without having to pay the full price of a plan.  I could leave for 3 months, return and activate my plan again.  Recently, they no longer offer this service.  This is so disappointing; now they are like all the other companies, so I will switch to Verizon or AT&T because both have better service and better coverage.  T-Mobile got rid of the one feature that kept the thousands of customers in my situation.  So sad.  They taken away the reason to stay with them, and I have been with them for 12 years now!  Goodbye T-Mobile!

Switched from Sprint to T-mobile 1.5 years ago. Got the BOGO S9+ deal and the 2 lines of 55+. Phones won't work reliably in the house on the network so we have to use wifi calling. Still drops sometimes trying to switch over.

  We have a lake house 75 miles away. Sprint would do texting and generally do calls. Sometimes 3G data on a good day in the right location. T-mobile nothing at all ever. ATT will do call and texting with data fairly often and Verizon does texts calling and some time 4G data. 

Price is good at T-mobile and the service people are good. The coverage is awful. What good is a great price if the service is nonexistant? Switching back when my phone contract is paid.

Why were we charged for a couple weeks of service we weren't even using?? Had we known all of this we wouldn't have done this! Not to mention, twice I contacted support and twice I was inadvertently disconnected?? I'll go back to AT&T, never had an issue with them.

Called t-mobile and said the phone cost is 256 when I went to the store they told me the phone cost 499.00 wth how was the price doubled. I wasted my time going there and the store rep won't even call T-Mobile and said I needed to call T-Mobile myself  and purchase the phone over the phone. Store rep said The prices are different from store and online. T-mobile are bunch of liars and all they want is to gouge you on paying extra when you shouldn't have to.

I've subscribed to T-mobile nearly five years ago. My original phone bill was $35 a month aside from taxes.  I was provided a $10 discount which lowered my monthly bill to just $25 a month.  I used to be a Century Link customer.  T-mobile provided me with the cheapest service!

Been a customer  for over 10 yrs..Paid the bill on time..When the phone service  I received  got so horrible..I switched  to a better carrier..Now they are getting  a full month charge because  they claim you have to give them a 30 day notice..If you cancel 1 day after the billing cycle begins.You are screwed...And are required  to pay all of it with no pro ration..Another 
cut throat company that charges you for service you are not receiving...And they get away with.!!! Pay alittle more for better.Stay far away from this carrier because they will screw you royally..IF I COULD GIVE TMOBILE A NEGATIVE 12 I WOULD BECAUSE THEY RATE IT.

TMobile customer service used to be good but now they are worst than ever. I really wish I checked reviews before signing up. It's cool, I'll just take my business elsewhere. I WILL NEVER BE A TMOBILE CUSTOMER AGAIN!

Bad customer service with not much knowledge.  All I needed was a replacement SIM for my existing prepaid account.  

1) I first went to a Costco kiosk because that's where I went last time I needed a free replacement SIM.  I was told to go to a retail store this time.

2) I went to a retail store.  I was told that it cost $25 (if not getting a new phone from them), but that it was free if I called Customer Service.

3) I called Customer Service.  I was transferred twice for no reason, and in the end I was told it was $10.

4) I tried online chat.  Now it went up to $25 again.  When I mentioned a SIM is sold for $10 on their own website, she said, "That's for a new account."  What difference does that make?  A new SIM is used for both new and existing accounts!  I had to end the chat by saying, "This is not worth my time or yours.  Good bye."

In the end, I ordered a new SIM on Amazon.  I'm glad this is just our backup phone.  We have a MUCH better postpaid phone service with Ting whose customer service is always excellent.
Absolutely horrible coverage. Lured in by freebies they offer but not worth the cheap prices and HORRIBLE coverage.

$287 for my monthly bill..... i have two phones and a watch on this plan and it should only cost me about $90-$100. i call go find out what the deal is and I'm told it's bc of all the services I have...understood, so I cancel the insurance for everything but apparently I'm still paying monthly for a sync drive that I ”won” at the rodeo. i told the lady I understand I was paying for the service but I had temporarily cancelled it until I can get a steady income. She said ”yes but if you cancel the service, you still have to pay for the device”.....are you serious!!! So I didn't win anything bc I still have to pay for it. That's what I'm REALLY MAD about

I am so disappointed in T-Mobile.  I've been a customer of theirs every since the 1990s.  For some reason someone took my insurance off my phone, without noticing me.  My phone cost over $700. They said I had no protection insurance on my phone.  Thats  was so crazy.  I always enjoyed their company.  But I  need a phone.

This company will scam you with hidden fees. Just like others here I got scammed in a couple thousand euro billed for services I have never used. Just stay away from it. It's better to pick a company that costs 2-3 euro more than being billed for thousands of euros in a single month.

Really if they had an option for zero stars that's what they'd get. One star is to much credit! My wife had just got home from serving our country in March an I'd just bought a house, unfortunately I had to switch cell phones providers being we didn't get service with them in our home. In March after i dropped t mobile I constantly called them for our final bill. For months they kept telling me it's being processed it's being processed. But when I was using their service I got billed on time and I paid on time. In the mist of me calling all this time asking for a final bill t mobile was sending out 10 dollar, 25 dollar bills every month. Like in lm not your customer any more, one big finally bill then id like to be done with you. Minding we put money aside for what we think it might've been. Unfortunately  we had death in the family that took everything we had to pay for the funeral. Almost half a year later still no final bill. It's like they want me to keep them in relationship of something😠.  One TMobile associate told me not to worry about that bill that it would just be dropped, on a recorded call. Weeks later t mobile calls again talking bout this bill. They went way back to March concerning recorded conversations, but wouldn't go listen to the last thing they had promised me. This company is disorganized and has no integrity of their word at all! Stay far away from this company please.

I was told about a promotion I liked it and accepted however when I called to find out why my bill was higher than it had been the error was fixed. The promotion was clarified for me, I was told what my bill would be moving forward and I got off the phone feeling good about being a Tmobile customer.That was last month. This months bill there were overcharges. I called and had an entirely different experience. I was told their was "nothing they could do." I did not understand what they were talking about! I then was told the promo was different than what I understood it to be. What I was being told made it obvious I didn't even qualify for the promotion! Now my bill has increased substantially. T mobile is not willing to offer me appropriate customer service or fix this. During the promo pitch the term "port line" was used. I had never heard this term. You know the saying, "you don't know what you don't know?'" I thought I understood. I thought it meant I'd get a third line. I think saying port line is shop talk and assuming people know what that is, is just wrong. I was not properly "qualified" to see if this promo was a fit! A simple question could  have been, "do you have a phone with another carrier?' would have sufficed and made all the difference! I had been with Tmobile for years! A good customer service person would have been able to deduct from the age of my now former phone with them that I was a Tmobile customer! Because I didn't know the "shop talk" "port line" meant transferring a phone number from another carrier, I am now literally paying for it... every month for a few years. I am now very confused as to why the month before my bill was corrected and this month its a different story. The manager I spoke with said I might have "remorse" and she really couldn't do anything for me. Not appropriate! I called to fix my bill! Where does remorse come in? There have been too may hands in this pot. I am confused and am looking for a new carrier.
T-Mobile SUCKS, they never lose, they make changes to your account without notifying and steal your money in every little way the can... screw that company 🖕

I would put no stars but that isn't an option. From the moment we got T-Mobile we had nothing but issues with the company. First the sales reps lied to us about the service we were getting and when we went back to the store they had been replaced by new people who had no idea what was going on. Then our bill went up EVERY SINGLE MONTH. And we had to talk to multiple people and we got the run around every time we tried to fix the problem. To make matters worse, when we moved to another state that did not have T-mobile they did not cancel our service after confirming that they did and put us in collections. To add insult to injury we paid off all the equipment fees and we just discovered they neglected to unlock our old phones. If your thinking about going with T-mobile don't do it.

I have three phones in one iPads the iPad went bad and had it less than 3 months and what I called to have it replaced they want $50 and they did not tell me anything about this so I am going to cancel all four lights at the set of August 17th I go back to the cricket or some other company that does not lie to you that customers

T-Mobile has got to be the worst cellular company out there not only did they lie to me they electronically sign papers that I didn't agree to an I never seen they went as far as to electronically sign me to a finance agreement on a telephone that they told me was free and every month that I had their service I had to call and talk to one of their so-called experts to get my bill straightened out because it was never even close to what it was supposed to be and then at the end I talked to one of their so-called experts and told them to shut my phone off when my prepaid account runs out of money so they do that and then send me a bill saying I owe them $233.89 and that's just how ridiculous they are and you cannot trust them they will lie they will sign your name to stuff that you did not want them to which I know is it illegal but how do you prove it that's what I don't know I wish I did cuz I would love to take him to court and make him stop these practices so if you deal with T-Mobile is all I have to say is good luck watch your bill they will lie cheat and steal to get your money

So - I travel internationally for most of my year and am a US citizen.  I bought an unlocked Samsung which is ran by T-mobile.  Last year when I left the US - I had no issue changing to the next countries SIMS card....but the year - I have been stranded in Portugal with no ability to use my phone for the first 48 hours of being here.  Evidently they lock your phone when you purchase a prepaid SIMS card now - and didn't bother telling me this.  I don't agree that they need to lock a phone at all with prepay - but let's just say it's okay - to NOT TELL YOUR CUSTOMER your doing so - well, that just sets them up for some very scary situations when they reach another country! We all rely upon the internet now'a'days to get us around a new town in the US - let alone a whole new country where you may or may not know the language.  So after 3 hours of phone calls - being put through the same questions over and over again until they could get me to the right department to finally unlock my phone - I was told I had to wait 72 hours! In the next 72 hours I will be traveling to two other cities in Portugal without the support of a working phone to help me make sure I feel secure about my travels.....not being able to reach out  to the AirBnB's I am renting - as I await this crappy company to unlock a phone that was already unlocked in the first place.  Not cool - transparency to your customers about their own PAID FOR PHONES should be a must on your list of attributes.  Never again TMobile.

I opened a acct with t.mobile ..telling the customer service employee that i wanted PRE PAY..WHICH MEANS MONTH TO MONTH SERVICE...PERIOD! THEY CSR THEN SHOWED ME A PHONE THAT WAS ON SALE FOR ..SHE SAID $50.00..I. ACCEPTED THE PHONE ON SALE..AFTER A MONTH OF SERVICE I FOUND THAT THE EMPLOYEE SIGNED ME UP FOR A CONTRACT INSTEAD..I THEN CHANGED CARRIERS. ...after a few months i decided thatt the phone i purchased from t mobile ..i liked better and tried to get it to work with my other carriers sims card.
.i called t mobile and they said my bill is outstanding...WHAT A RIP OFF...I ONLY ASKED FOR MONTH TO MONTH SERVICE...AND NOW I ALSO HAVE A PHONE THAT THEY ARE CHARGEING ME FOR ..THAT WAS AGREED TO BE A "ON SALE "ITEM...
I AM A ETHICAL PERSON ..I DONT CARE FOR LIARS...AND I WAS LIED TO AND NOW THEY ARE STEALING FROM ME...SAYING I OWE ON A LEASED PHONE...
HOW CAN THEY JUST DO THIS TO PEOPLE AND GET BY WITH IT..O WILL CONTACT SOMEONE THAT WILL HELP THE NEWS MAYBE.. BUT THAT IS WRONG..TOTALLY WRONG...THE EMPLOYEES NEED TO BE TRAINED TO REPRESENT T MOBILE...NOT STEAL FOR THEM
TMobile decides for the customers it won’t sell Samsung Foldable phone second time around. The excuse you provided to the Verge is a bunch of crap!

T-mobile is worse than Amazon or Comcast with regard to customer service. They should have a zero rating. Tried to talk to a customer service in the USA and all I got was the PI who could not answer my simplest question including could I talk to a manager. Obviously management is not available . How does this company stay in business ? "A representative will be with you shortly". T-Mobile used to have company stores with fairly average services, now most of the stores appear to be branded which means independent operations ,the main goal being upgrading review but not service.

I was a loyal T-Mobile customer for 18 years.  I had continually seen ads on TV for new plans, which included a new phone.  Each time I asked T-Mobile what the incentives were for existing customers, I was told they do not offer incentives for existing customers.  As such, I recently changed carriers and bought a new phone from the new carrier.  
It took me two weeks to get my phone changed over from T-Mobile to the new carrier.  They provided the worst customer service by providing incorrect and incomplete information.
1.  First, they did not tell me that you had to have your phone unblocked before you switch carriers  Three people told me all I needed was the account number, phone number and security code.  This was a lack of disclosure, which resulted in delays.
2.  When we finally discovered that this was not communicated, we were told it would take 48 hours to get the phone unblocked.  It actually takes 72 hours.  This was incorrect information, resulting in yet another delay.
3.  My account was paid up to date, but I got an e-mail telling me they could not unblock my phone because I had pending charges.  I should call back when I have paid the charges.  Wrong information, I had no outstanding balance.  This resulted in another delay.
4.  I called the service team and asked for a manager.  I was told that I could make a payment but without the security code, which I no longer had as I did not know it would take me 2 WEEKS of talking to T-Mobile.  I told them, I can't make a payment until you tell me the amount.  I was finally told that since the account was closed, they disclose the balance.  One again, an opportunity to provide a delay in changing carriers, 
5.  The manager then told me there was no balance due.  When I asked why I got the e-mail, she indicated it must be a mistake, 
My phone was finally unlocked two days later.  If unlocking a phone is so important and starts the process, they should disclose that type of information.
Absolutely terrible service.  Front line service people do not know the process, can't give consistent information, and can't actually help you.  Store associates are no better.  Managers, if you can get to one, have no idea what their teams are actually doing or saying 
If you are shopping for a new carrier, I would not recommend this one.

I had something of a phone crisis on my hands, and I went to four T-Mobile stores covering three cities and two states to fix the problem. The only store that truly managed to help me resolve the situation with true professionalism from the staff, was the store on Campbell, south of Ft. Lowell, on the east side of the street in Tucson. The staff was so far superior than the other three stores that I will now only go to them from here on out. They were all courteous, professional, didn't try to sell me something I didn't need (honest), and friendly. The store is slightly difficult to find because it's set in a little south off of Campbell, but there is a pink T-Mobile flag on the street. I highly recommend this store to any T-Mobile customer in Tucson. I'm a teacher and that store gets an "A."

I've been a T-Mobile customer for 12 years.  Basically I used my phone, paid the bill, didn't have any real issues.  I went into the store every couple years for a new phone, and that went fine, if slow.  Then I decided to switch plans to take advantage of their military plan. Wow, it's been the worst customer experience ever.  I spent 90 minutes in the store...even their own representatives could not get through to customer service.  After an hour they had an epiphany and said "wow, we can take care of this in the store on the computer."  THAT shouldn't have taken an hour to figure out.  No go, though, as their computers turned out to be "down."  I come back home, call customer service myself, find out that all the information I was given in the store was wrong and I have to be transferred to the main customer service number.  90 minutes on hold and nobody came on the line.  Guess I'm going to be switching CARRIERS instead of plans.  Totally unacceptable.  That's what 12 years of loyalty gets you...three wasted hours and no help.

Nothing seems to work. Their app doesn't work on the phone and their website fails to run on firefox and chrome without errors in loading the pages. Never had such a difficult experience with a phone contract.

As a full time rver. We though we would give Tmobile a try. There cheaper then Verizon. We got 2 lines in Washington state and were driving back to our home state of Maine. We live in Northern Maine by the Canadian border.
Long story short this company can not even coming close to what Verizon covers. We roamed alot of the way back and in alot of areas we had zero service. We have done this trip many times before with Verizon and never had an issue. We got home and I looked at my phone and it said no service. We told the Tmobile rep in Washington state that we lived in remote northern Maine and he's like we definitely have service there. Well they don't. Ended up switching back to Verizon and I was not able to return the phones because Tmobile told me that I had to return them to the store I got them. This company is nothing but a scam
Just had the best experience with Iza from tmobile, very informative, sweet, patient and friendly. Wat to go Iza with your customer service skills!!!

They charged me (unauthorized) my credit card 3 months and never refunded me. They said the payments were made with cash and not with a credit card. What the hell?

After a month trying to get my refund, I called my bank and they blocked T-Mobile charges. Customer service never did their job properly. Every time I called there was something "Missing" to get my refund.

A company that doesn't care about its customers AT ALL

I don't recommend it at all!
This company is a BIC SCAM and the federal gov has to investigate them they promise you for something than you find out they do something different  just to scam you . its time to STOP them?/

This T-Mobile needs to have a large class action suit. They are unable to deliver Adequate service to their Customers. I have  medical issues during telephone calls my calls were dropped and  I tried to have a normal conversation but I could not Then at times I would have to recall the number several times to get it to connect for it to ring. I’m so disappointed with T-Mobile. I absolutely dread calling for help. The hold time is extremely long and exhausting. I’ve been on the phone for over 2 hrs trying to get help. It’s so sad that the company charges their customers so much to not to receive poor customer service.
Horrible customer service. Regret even calling
This got to be the worst phone company in History.  My account was close and they kept charging me money.

Don’t use t-Mobile. It has been an absolute nightmare. I should have known when I saw that all the employees were under the legal drinking age. I signed up with these teenagers because they quoted their bills lower than Verizon AND they assured me that I would have coverage on my entire 1-hour work commute. They sold me a device for my car to increase coverage on my route. However, the device was actually intended to provide a wifi hot spot in your car, but only if there was t-Mobile coverage in the area. My commute picked up t-Mobile coverage for less than a third of the trip. Not only did they give me faulty information, but they failed to tell me that I only had 14 days to change my mind. I returned to t-Mobile a month later to close my account and go back to Verizon. Regardless of the fact that they were dishonest to get me to sign up with them (I asked, and yes he got a commission for signing folks on), they adhered to the 14 day period. I was forced to buy out my contract, totaling nearly $1,000. After I fulfilled my commitment to them, it still took six months of angry phone calls to finally be rid of this awful company. They kept trying to charge me for services I was no longer receiving and withhold money owed me. Some people just don’t know how to run a successful business.

If even one star.  I am a LONG time t mobile customer but as the rates go up the service is getting worse.  I used to really like them but am now going to AT &T after the way I’ve been treated.  Bad service, empty promises, increased fees... this is ludicrous.

These people SUCK. My bill used to be the same every single month as it should be. The last 6 months though (since upgrading and adding a line) my bill has done nothing but yo-yo. It is not consistent and I constantly have to call and fight with "my expert" on why my bill is yet again screwed up. If I wanted this type of "service" I would switch back to Verizon. So with that in mind I am shopping around to other cell phone companies as T-Mobile clearly doesn't care about the 4 years I have put up with them. Save yourself the headache and go elsewhere.
If I could give 0 start I would do. This copany is the worse I have ever known. Customer service is horrible. Nobody seems to know anything.

In wish you had a choice for zero stars!  I signed up with T-mobile less than two weeks ago, and the day after I signed up my contract was changed.  I was given the excuse that the plan I had signed up for had expired and I was switched to a new plan.  (Btw, I was told by customer service that the deal I was signed up for never existed). The new plan was not acceptable because it would no longer pay off one of the phones I still owed AT&T for, and without that payoff I cannot afford to leave AT&T.  I asked for my old phones back and was told that they where the property of TMobile and I had no right to them because I surrendered them.  True I surrendered them, but It was to an offer that they broke/changed without my knowledge.  Now I am getting the run around between the store and customer care.  The store people treat you nice but no action has been taken so far and I’ll be running out of my 14 day return window in two days.  I entered into a contract with them in good faith and after being a loyal customer to AT&T for almost 20 years, I just wanted to save some money on my monthly bill so I could pay off a hospital bill, I was even willing to live with the bad signals and dropped calls. I have lost all faith and trust in this company. I have not slept in days and have had numerous panic attacks because of it, but of course they don’t care about that.
All I am asking them right now is to have the contract cancelled without charging me for the return fees and either give back my two old phones or give me the amount I owe AT&T for the phones so I can go back and start from scratch with AT&T.  Either way I will be loosing a lot of money that I cannot afford to lose, but at least I would be able to sleep again and not have anymore panic attacks.
I will post an update whenever things get resolved and I still have have hope that I will be able to add more stars to my review once they admit to their errors and make things right.
I have absolutely nothing good to say about T-Mobile what a waste of time and money!!!!!

Tmobile was charging me for a line that I didn’t know that existed and I didn’t ask  for it.Worse than that I was asking for a refund of 10 months of pay and they want to refund for two months.  Please make sure to read what you sign at the store or online.  Some people likes to do comisión in a customer’s back. I am leaving the company.
They sux and they bill in arrears which makes NO sense to me at all!!

We were loyal customers for years. Then my husbands job started paying for our phones so we decided to cancel service once our newest contract was up. We canceled in October 2018. Called in, paid the service in full and even paid off our phones. Jump to May 2019 when we decided to sale our home and buy a new one. Pull or credit to find an outstanding balance. We called and were told that our service ran until February 2019 and then was disconnected for non payment. We have been fighting with customer service ever since. They even agreed that they wouldn’t have released our phone numbers if we hadn’t paid everything in full and yet they still refuse to remove it from our credit and demand payment for service from October to February. I always spoke highly of them and only canceled because carrying 2 phones was stupid. I’m at a point where I feel I need to contact an attorney.

You would be better off with Cricket. I've had TMobile for 8 years. Their coverage gets worse every year. I live and work in the Denver metro area and I have serviceable coverage maybe 40-50% of the time and that's being liberal. The service at my house makes my phone utterly unusable. I'm constantly sending texts, FB messages, and snaps that are reporting to me as sent but the recipient never gets them. Disappearing texts, lost voicemails, and dropped calls occur almost daily. I also travel alot and don't get reception in most large cities but when im at the top of a 14er I'm the only one on the mountain with service! I've tried every level of tech support there is and they are literally telling me they've tried everything. TMobile is excellent at having service in basements and on top of mountains but if you live near the slightest hint of civilization save your money and go sign up with the Walmart phone carrier because anything has better coverage than tmobile.

Update 7.5.19
I still don’t have a phone! On 7.2.19 I took my nonworking phone to a local T-mobile store in Centreville, They couldn’t get it to power on and determined that I needed a replacement, under my warranty plan. The employee couldn’t access my account because there was an problem with my account and sent me home. Yet I had received an email a few days prior that confirmed the account was in good standing and a payment had been received. I call my wife at work after returning home, she calls customer service, who proceeds to tell me that the store employee should’ve called them to verify or access the account. After giving them a description customer service decides to send me a replacement phone to the local store within 2 days. Later that afternoon my wife gets a confirmation text that the phone was sent. The next day, 7.3.19, my wife receives a text that the phone arrived, and was received at 12 noon. Not knowing this I call the store at 3.30p to see if it’s in so I can pick it up, the employee tells me that it hasn’t arrived. I see my wife around 530p and she shows me the text, along with a second text confirming the delivery. She calls the store and they confirm that its there. I go there around 6.30p, the employee finds it but says she can’t release it because the manager didn’t check it in and he needs to be there to release the phone. I asked what time did he leave, she says 11a and will be in at 10a the next morning? So I go home again without a phone. The next morning, 7.4.19, I return at 10a, the manager’s not there but the employees find, unpackage the phone, and calls the manager to find a way to release it, which takes about 30 minutes. 

Next a glitch occurs, concerning if I should be allowed to keep my original phone. Since it was replaced by the customer service, one employee says that I can take it, while the other says I have to turn it in to get the replacement? She calls the manager to confirm! I explain that I need to transfer my data, business contacts, notes, etc, and since they can’t do it there, I can take it to a business that can, and after I’ll return it. My wife’s did this with hers when T-mobile and the Apple store couldn’t retrieve her data, and she returned the broken phone afterwards. So we were at a stalemate, with both employees sympathizing with me, but the manager stating the store policy. So one employee proceeds to accesses the company policy to review, then calls someone and they say that I can’t have the phone because of an outstanding balance.  Also, that it has to be returned, and reordered. So after an hour I leave again without a phone. 

 Before I leave I express my disappointment, frustration, and anger that T-mobile’s customer service is horrible! Why would I get an confirmation email and customer service process the order if the account wasn’t in good standing. I have my own business, so besides clients not being able to reach me nor I them, my 89 year old dad who I care for was in the hospital, 5 weeks, and I was in communication with the doctors, nurses, social worker, caregivers, etc, daily. I had to authorize his care and procedures. So T-mobile’s inept customer service cost me valuable time, money, ability to care for my dad, and manage my business. 
 
T-mobile needs to review their customer service policy and procedures! The current system is broken. Local managers and employees need the ability to provide service that’s efficient and effective. Again, this is the 5th phone replacement in 2 & ½ years! Someone should’ve stepped in to immediately resolve it. Everyone agreed that 5 phone replacements wasn’t good service but no one could resolve it in a consumer friendly and efficient manner. My wife spent a few hours arguing and trying to resolve this. Talking to managers and supervisors yet no one had the authority or urgency to resolve it! She told them what to do or what they should do to get it fixed, yet after knowing the circumstances no one could resolve it in a timely manner. 

Horrible, customer service! I own a small business and I could have resolved this, instantly. If I had provided a customer with 5 bad products, I would’ve bent over backwards to appease that customer! Including asking them what, how, where, and when or whatever they need to resolve this. I would have sucked it up and provide great customer service to make up for the previous experience! At this time T-mobile can’t do that! No one on the family plan wants any part of this plan or with  this kind of service, actually non-service!

T-Mobile Complaint, 7.2.19

I have two disputes with T-mobile. The first, since signing up in December of 2016, I'm about to start only 5th phone (4th replacement phone). The initial new sore phone was a LG G4, which the volume went bad after a year. When the replacement came I unboxed it in the store and the representative notice that the screen was defective, so she took it back and ordered another one. Each order takes about 2-3 business days, so I'm without business contacts etc...the new phone arrives LG G5 and they transfer my data within 60 minutes. Well this phone goes bad over a year later, so they order a replacement which takes 3 days. I spend over 3 hours in the store with them trying to set it up (system delay problems) and difficulty transferring my data which they failed to complete, so I lost half of my data. A little over a year later and that phone suddenly goes black, and overheats, but won't restart or charge. Now they are sending me another phone but they should be letting me pick out the phone of my choice from within the store, instead of sending me a turned in, refurbished, or reconditioned phone. I still have original cell phones from 2000, from several other carriers that aren't broken just outdated.

My second issue maybe an administrative issue. In 2016 I was convinced to leave my provider (Sprint Monthly Plan, with my own phone), along with my In-Laws (Verizon), and daughter (new), to join T-mobiles family plan with my wife (Virgin), her brother (T-mobile) and his son (T-mobile). The sales pitch was we would save money, get updated phones, and other perks. So there's 7 of us on this plan. What no one told us that if any of us are late, they disconnect everyone’s service, then charges a $20 reconnection fee for each line or individual, $140. So we've accumulated a sharecroppers indebtedness of reconnect penalties, which we didn't realize initially because the service would be restored for the monthly minimum, but the debt remained. Once each occurrence $140 extra would accumulate to the bill. Trying to coordinate everyone to know and be instinct with their potion of the bill payment is ridiculous, which I told a representative while trying to understand and resolve the bill. Yet I couldn't because only my wife and her brother were administrators to the account. Everyone wants to leave T-moble but feels trapped or locked in because of the outstanding penalties. I've never been involved in system like this, I've had Sprint, T-mobile Monthly, Straight Talk, & Sprint Monthly. My wife says she's tried to negotiate with them, and threaten to leave but they've refused. It's a share cropping system that locks users in so they can't leave without resolving the debt!

I forgot to mention that my In-Laws phones are going bad also! We all had the insurance replacement plans which adds $6-$12 per month per phone to the plan. I never had nor needed an insurance plan with my previous plans!

If I could write the review without a selecting a star I would have. Since 2016 I've been mad and still am now in 2019. At the end of this month (July) I'll be switching. Every year as soon as our phones reaches close to a year they stopped working. This is the cycle. We get a software update, then phones begin to freeze, internet slow as hell, not able to retrieve incoming calls. The whole nine yard. You know what's T mobile's fix for the problem on their devices after claiming it's Samsung's issue? We'll send you a BRAND NEW PHONE or YOU CAN COME OUT OF POCKET WITH X AMOUNT FOR DOWNPAYMENT FOR  THE NEXT HIGHER LEVEL PHONE.  At no point in time did we receive new phones, always refurbished ones. In 2016 a total of seven phones (s7's) were mailed to me. Each time I spent hours on the phone with T mobile they trying to troubleshoot the BRAND NEW PHONE they just mailed to me. I wasted time transferring data from one phone to the next. Never got done properly as not everything got transferred over. Next were the trips to the store to have someone there did what I did with the same damn result. Mind you this doesn't have to me only, my husband and niece as well. The never ending problems vary from T Mobile crossing the IMEI numbers for me and my husband's phone. They mailed me a phone but on their system it's my husband's phone should be returned them. All these trips to the post office to mailed back phones were also ridiculous as the stores were no longer hallowed to accept them. The biggest thing of all are the text messages we began to receive about we failed to return phones. After reciting the tracking number on our receipts. This rubbish continued and on occassion we went in store to buy something only to be told our phone bill was $1400 plus. A 
Missing was being charged to our account. The tracking Information that was provided to T Mobile even stated the name of the signature of the person who received the phone, but oh forgot to log it in their system. These are the nonsense that we've been putting up with from T mobile. Now tonight took the cake. My husband's and my phones dont work unless we turned WiFi on. Now granted our payments are up to date. Always been. After being on the phone with T Mobile 5 hours and counting we were told to get different sim cards and insert in both phones. Then we were told to continue using the phones on Wifi until tomorrow when they T Mobile will call us back. Now I need Gps, my husband needs GPS, he also have customers calling  him throughout the day. How do we keep the phones connected to our wifi while working on the road. At this point I got really upset  because even though our problems weren't solved we we being rushed off the call. I guessed the T Mobile expert were tired now after six hours and counting. That's two friggin hours less than my hours at work. Then an amazing offer was made,  refurbished phones or $560 downpayment to go get S10's. We didn't tell T Mobile nor anyone as a matter of fact that we wanted S10 phones. After clearly stating that over and over and asking for a manager  over and over we were told that if we get a manager that offer would no longer be on the table. We didnt stuttered when we said we don't want your damn S10'S. We were placed on hold for 32 minutes before someone came on line. The process was repeated 2 more friggin times each time the hold time longer. At this point my husband and I agreed that we will be switching network. The refurbished phones should be reached us by this Wednesday. You know something the manager asked my husband to please give them a good rating on a survey that would be sent to us. Got to be F@#*ing kidding me. On this call a whole lot more were discovered from: insurance on my phone was dropped In November of last year,  but every damn payment we made insurance amount was included as well as stated on the bill. How the hell can so many things be wrong?

I signed up with T-Mobile back in December 2018 during a time where they had a promotional offer of a buy one get one on them iPhone XR with eligible trade-in. The agent I spoke with to sign up for T-Mobile screwed me over and did not include in the order that I would be trading in phone. Come January, I call again to Customer Service and get this run around that they screwed up the order and that they would credit my account the amount of the phone which would go towards the monthly payment of the free phone once I sent in my old one.
 
I called today because I received my bill and it was higher than what I expected and the balance on the phone that should have been free was still over $600. The agent explained to me, much more clearly this time, that the credit was towards free service instead of the phone. In essence I got free service for 6 months, which is true since I did not make any payments, but it was NOT explained to me that way because I would not have agreed to that. I expected them to put the credit towards the free offer to "honor the promotion." What a bunch of garbage!! I switched from Verizon to T-Mobile which was a BIG mistake. T-Mobile service is already sub-par but the customer service is quite flawed!!

If you asked me last week i would probably give 5 stars. But all of a sudden I get a $200 charge in my account and tries to figure out what it was. I once ordered a receptor because I didn’t have coverage while living in manhattan!!! I never returned it because it got lost and this is supposed to be the value of it! I called customer service and they first lied to me saying I would get a refund (which i never got) and when I called back they were useless. Would not recommend T-mobile at all. No coverage in one of the biggest city in the world and bad customer service. Sorry, it has the potential but with that culture will never be at the other companies level.

And I thought Sprint Customer Service was bad.

I had a card compromised and had to get a new card number around the time of T-Mobile's billing.  I understand it was my responsibility to change it but I overlooked it and got dinged for a late payment.

So, I called and spoke to a dingbat who supposedly fixed it and thought all was well until just now when I received a text saying I was delinquent again. I had assumed everything was good with my account until today.  

So naturally, I called again and after speaking with another customer service expert, I was stoked for having to hear the difference between "auto pay" and a "pay arrangement" which I still do not understand.  For what it's worth a payment did not go through.  She says it's all fixed now but had to listen to a warning that I would lose my auto-pay discount if it did not go through again.  What?  That was the whole issued in the first place.  I trusted that it would be fixed.

I would gladly pay a year at a time not to have to deal with these people.

That pretty much explains why my credit score fell from 822 to 820.

We have had t mobile for over 10 years. My Mom recently passed. We had 3 lines. Dropped my Moms. Then had to change over my Brothers for his was being covered. I was told my Bill would be $65 a month. I even received a call a week later saying again $65 a month. Last 3 bills are still $93 which was the old rate. Nice of the companies employees lying to someone that lost their Mom. I switched to Verizon and already get better service in areas I did not. Saved &65 a month. Bought a new phone and spoke with t mobile and was going to use other phone for some else’s line. T mobile said no problem it will be unlocked in 24 hours. 3 days later nothing. Of course the Lady from t mobile I spoke with was so nasty. Glad I am no longer with them.

I wish there was an option for 0 stars. 

This is my husbands and mines review for tmobile and how they treated us...let this be a warning to everyone who has them or those who are wanting to go to them...the monthly payments are lower there then Verizon BUT it's not worth it...

Our experience with TMobile has definitely been a horrible one. In late February early March 2018 we decided to change our cell services from Verizon to Tmobile 🙄 which was the worst mistake ever. Every single week for the first and second month I was on their chat trying to fix mistakes they made to our bill each month. In fact before we received our first bill I was already on their message chat trying to fix something that shouldn't have been on our account. Our bill amounts were never the same and after telling them several times that both phones had the kick back one thing I can't remember the name of that exactly, they never fixed it and ended up charging us a bill for over 300 dollars. We were on vacation at the time and the last thing I should've been doing on my vacation was worrying about their stupid mistakes. We unfortunately stayed with them until March of 2019 when we paid almost 700 dollars to pay off our phones and we cancelled our services with them. It took us 4 hours to get them to cancel our services, especially the people via phone the people over chat were more willing to help us. But still, FOUR HOURS to cancel. Then 3 months later they send us a bill for 385 dollars in over due fees 😠😤 saying that it was on my phone line. In between those 3 months we have been with Verizon and have new phone numbers and not once did we get a tmobile bill in the mail saying we still had a monthly payment, we also sold our phones that we had with tmobile so there was no service on either of the phones because gazelle.com doesn't allow phones that have service still on them. My wife even would check online sporadically to see if anything popped up just incase. So we got the over due bill fixed and only had to pay 65 dollars (I was tired of fighting with them so I just paid it I know I really shouldn't have but I was fed up and wanted it over and done) The lady helping us told us she cancelled everything and that we were fine. Today they sent us a bill for 5 bucks for late payment and that was the final straw for us on being nice. We know that it is only 5 bucks BUT we were told there was nothing else we needed to do or pay and that we would be getting a final bill of 0 dollars. After the crap they put us through for a whole year and they ask us for 5 more dollars when they never sent us a bill for 3 month?! Sounds like this company just wants money and cares nothing about the people they serve. If you have tmobile or thinking about getting tmobile I would suggest finding something else. They might be good for some people, but I would hate for anyone to go through what we went through. Stay away from tmobile. 

Let me also say that the lady I talked to last week about the 385 told me it was because we didn't pay the final bill back in March, which was $130, we were told that it would be sent via mail and we looked out for it and it never came. That's when my wife started looking online to see if it was there and it was not. Instead they wait 3 months and try to pull the wool over our eyes. Thank goodness we didn't sign up for automatic payments, who knows how much money they would've taken from us.

T mobile is AWESOME. The reps are always friendly and professional. I was just just speaking with Zairel. She took care of my issue, and explained to me everything that she did to do it. Thank you Zairel!!

Have had T-mobile for over 15 years and it has gotten so bad I am quitting once my contract is over, I would quit now but I'm stuck. Contract for "One 
All In" with jump on demand for 2 lines.  Contract was not explained correctly when I signed it. Was supposed to be around $100 with phones, I am paying over $200 a month! I don't even get to keep the phones that I have been paying for over the past 18 months unless I pay additional money (over $400 for 2 phones) at the end of the contract. I have spent MANY HOURS (MORE THAN  6) on the phone with their support because contract was not correct and items were not available and/or delivered. Support did cover my bill for one month, at least showing effort, but it just hasn't been worth the effort, time, and grief on my side. 

My daughter has one of the lines and is near Indianapolis IN, and she is constantly dropping calls and having reception problems. She wants to move to different carrier, but we are stuck in this contract, and she can't use her phone unless I pay additional money (over $200) when the contract is finished. I also am required to pay for insurance for the phones, or am required to pay for any damages incurred, even though I don't own the phones. 

It states all over their website and also under my account, that Netflix is included for free with the One Plus program. I am told it doesn't include me because I have this "Jump on Demand" option. When I login to my account, it says it is included with One Plus, it says I have  One Plus, it has this on multiple pages! But again I am told it doesn't apply to me because if Jump on Demand. I don't want this Jump on Demand,  but am stuck with no way out. 

My hotspot is getting turned off and made unavailable, and my data speed is made slower because I use to much data. This was put in a notification I received.  I am supposed to have unlimited data.

It says I have thousands of HD minutes to use but it doesn't retain the selection to use it in my profile. Therefore, regardless of how many minutes of it I have, I can't use them.

They updated the Visual Voicemail so that you have to use theirs instead of the native application. I received over 20 voicemails and was unaware because their app didn't notify me. The visual voicemail and regular voicemail no longer syncs, and I am not notified when I have a regular message. 

I feel like I was conned into this contract so they could get my money, the phones have bad reception, I am not getting the data speed and amounts I am supposed to, and I am stuck without even owning the phones when its over. T-mobile sucks. After 14 years, I have had enough and I'm done getting scammed by their offers that are misleading and aren't what they seem to be. False advertising!
I have a issue with the supervisor by name of clarence

T-Mobile for over 10 years. I pay my bill religiously early every month. Antonio m empoyee Id 1267372 tampa call center in Florida is amazing he did all that he could to help me and he was a total God sent I so love him. I'm so appreciative of him thank u tmobile for hiring him I dont even know him but I'm so thankful great job with that one guys

T-Mobile is a pice of dookie I am being charged for a signal booster that I returned 6 years ago my house was involved in a fire and I do not have the receipt from Staples where I shipped it from. The first person I talked to found it and said everything was ok. Then 3 years I get a letter saying I owe them 603$ for it again so I call back  and I am told they can't find it in the warehouse, it has hit my credit and is affecting me getting my house I have asked to speak to someone in the corporate office and never received a call from them so here is my number again call me because you are wrong for charging me for something I already returned 614-218-2550 Robert cephas

I am a T-Mobile customer from Daly City, California and frequent the Westlake store her. I just want to point out the outstanding personalized service I receive whenever I stop in. Glenn,Theo, Leonardo make a point to help me as quickly as possible as well as the other customers because this place is always busy. They are very professional, extremely knowledgable, understanding and they make you feel as welcome as a good friend. I am still recovering from a near fatal concussion a year ago and I must admit I can be difficult sometimes. This All Star team is sensitive to my condition and go the extra mile when serving me. Even when I was healthier and using other carriers I never felt so appreciated as a customer as I do with T-Mobile. To Sprint, Metro and Verizon I felt like a number and to them it was just all about the money. I am thrilled with my new T-Mobile tablet and their network provides faster than lightning speeds for my devices, no issues. Hip Store, music in the groove and a great staff! The Sprint stores inside always feel like you walked into a rental car place. No thanks.

In Germany, when you walk into a Tmobile store, make sure they ARE TMOBILE. Sometimes, they are only an exclusive partner. They use the Tmobile logo, and displays. But they are NOT Tmobile per se. When I walked into a store, they could not check my financial information for my account. This is extremely deceptive practice. 

If they were NOT Tmobile, then it should be displayed as their company name offering Tmobile services. I did not even know they allowed something like this.

I have been a loyal customer for years!!! I do not ask much of them but when my calls are dropping or i can’t utilize the apps and features how I’m supposed to. They want to blame it on my phone because it’s a little older.



Like if you can’t fix the problem just say that. Also i told the manger that i was very frustrated and was considering leaving the company if they cannot fix my problem. The supervisor stated to me that he understands my decision and stated that i basically have to do what i have to. 

Unbelievable, at this point i felt like they didn’t even care about me being a customer for years. Smh something has to change
Kendra from the Cleveland office did a fantastic job and was a total pleasure to work with. Thank you Kendra for the he fantastic customer service you you gave me today.

T-Mobile's Customer Support is HORRIBLE! What should have been a simple porting from Tmo to Tmo because of a plan change has turned into a crippled account with a phone # that Tmo only sporadically recognizes. Long story short, I've tried every option they provide for support but I only get the "We've opened a ticket" or "Wait 48-72 hours" or "I see they're still working on it" replies. All of these responses are lies. "Yes, yes, yes" from a cust svc rep when it's clear they're not understanding what you're describing is frustrating as all get out. There was a time when I would have recommended Tmo. Never again will I, and I wish I could give this review 0 stars!

I had a battery issue. called T-Mobile to change the battery. They couldn't change the battery but offered me a phone. They ordered the phone  to a closest T -mobile location . It was lakeview ave. Clifton , NJ store.  I walked in and greeted by a lady. She took my information and my phone. It seems that her main focus was the scrathes at the bottom of the phone. She told me that she can't exchange the phone. I asked for the reason and she looked at the phone more and told me that that was a crack on the screen. I told her that I don't see it as a crack. It is very tiny, small minor spot on the phone but she already made her predetermination!!!  I told her that I am happy with my phone and all I need to repleace a battery to not have to charge my phone for every 2-3 hours. She told me that I have to go through the insurance and pay deductable in order to get the phone. 

Here are my frustration and concerns:
I went the store to pick up the phone, I wan't expecting the store personnel be that much picky and inconsiderate and, not welcoming. 
If I go through insurance, I will be paying $100-150. 
Why are you behind my $100-150 ?  You already made enough money out of my pocket.  I purchased the phone from T mobile years ago paying $12-13 warranty each month on top of that. ( I paid about $1000 for warantly so far) So, I paid T- mobile more than enough. Stiil paying for warranty. Is that how you much you care about your loyal customer? It is absoletely ridicilous and unacceptable!!!

if $1000 additional cost will not cover any damage on the phone, Then pay my $1000 back? (With  $1000 , I can buy a brand new phone without going through this frustration)


I wanted to share my personal experince with T- mobile. I am rating T- Mobile service as 1 especiall they store on lakeview Ave. Clifton. ( I would give negative point , but such point doesn't exits).

I am completely disappointed by the service provided by T-mobile. I came to visit the US and was sold a SIM with 2GB of data for 22$, which is fair. The problem is the SIM was never meant to be used in a phone, so it got blocked after 120 MBs of data. After this no one could fix it, tried other T-mobile shops in Las Vegas. T-mobile customer service was absolutely useless!!! They wouldn't even understand what the issue was.
Also, while the data was working the coverage was ridiculous on my trip from LA to Las Vegas, Grand Canyon, Page, Bryce, etc. I DO NOT recommend this option to anyone. It is a waste of money and time. I will post this review on every possible travelling site.
Eduardo at the HIP, Chicago, IL has been amazing to my family, will refer my friends and family.

If I could give ZERO stars I would. This is hands down the absolute worst service I have had in 21 years of owning cell phones. Conviently, when I attempt to create a sign in for the mobile app that you have to use, I am told that I already have an account. Funny...I don't recall creating that account. But I play along and press "Forgot My Password." At which point I am told I must call Customer Service. Which is the last thing either them or myself want right now. HORRIBLE SERVICE!!

I was sold a device at a T-Mobile store after the T-Mobile call center offered me the current deal on an S10. The deal was confirmed at the store. I should have never trusted them when both the store and call center did not give me a signed contract for the sale. I was told it takes 1-2 billing cycles to receive the credits. After the buyers remorse period expired I was told that the deal was not valid and I would have to pay full price or send back the phone. To return it I was forced to TRUST them, as T-Mobile wouldn't send me anything in writing regarding the fees or refunds I would have to accept. Multiple supervisors refused to send me anything in writing. I am now forced to dispute the charges with my credit card company.

T-Mobile is a horrible company with unacceptable customer service. They will mislead, cheat, overcharge and lie to their customers, while assuring they’d take best care of you.  I’ve been loyal customer of T-Mobile for 15 years, and have had enough of their lies, overcharges, I’ve been with them for 12 yeas, when they took away my unlimited data plan and substituted it with 2 GB data plan, no explanation nothing! Completely unacceptable!  So they can force  me into $70/month plan - $20/month higher than I’ve been paying for many years. Today was my last day with T-Mobile.  I know… This should have happened way sooner. But it’s better later than never. I switched to Sprint and will be saving $45 per month! My plan is only $25 per month and has exact same features.
 Do not sign up with your t mobile. Avoid by all cost.
